title: Builder resume disposition (PRD gauntlet-resume-response, FR-10)
description: Machine-checkable outcome a builder emits at the end of every `gauntlet resume --response` re-evaluation; the deterministic test oracle for the FR-3.0 precedence and FR-5 outcomes, so tests assert on these fields, not on prose. The disposition enum maps 1:1 to the FR-3 categories: proceed_in_place/proceed_with_deviation complete the step (DONE then commit); amendment_required/new_conflict re-park it (parked_reason=upstream_conflict) for a human / the FR-10.4 gate. conflict is ALWAYS required at the top level (strict-mode compatibility) and is an object when the disposition re-parks, null when it proceeds. WHY NO allOf (BOOTSTRAP-NOTES #46): this schema is handed to the builder as NATIVE STRUCTURED OUTPUT on a --response resume, and the Anthropic API rejects a top-level oneOf/allOf/anyOf in a tool input_schema (`400 tools.N.custom.input_schema`). The object-vs-null discriminator therefore cannot be a schema-level allOf/if/then/else; it is enforced fail-closed by the engine (steptypes._conflict_shape_error, in _resume_disposition_result), together with the two other semantic rules the schema cannot express: responses_considered must name the consumed (pending) response and only known response_ids (FR-1/FR-5/FR-10), and conflict.artifact must be a non-empty string when disposition=amendment_required (FR-3(b)). Strict-mode note (mirrors findings.json, codex 0.139.0): native structured output requires EVERY property in `required`, so conflict is spelled required-but-nullable.

disposition.description: FR-3.0 classification of the latest response. proceed_in_place: conflict resolved through understanding, no artifact change. proceed_with_deviation: a deviation the approved artifacts already permit. amendment_required: the response requests, or proceeds despite, a divergence from approved PRD/plan text — routed back through the artifact's own gate (FR-3(b), no in-place amend). new_conflict: the response is ambiguous or impossible and does not resolve the conflict.

responses_considered.description: The response_id(s) (e.g. implement-resp-1) the builder consumed from human-response.md; the response(s) this disposition is a function of (FR-5/FR-10). Must be non-empty and must name the consumed (pending) response — the engine fails closed otherwise.

action_summary.description: One-line description of what the builder did or will do.

requested_input.description: What the human must supply or change next. For new_conflict, names what the supplied response did NOT provide (FR-5) — not a verbatim repeat of the prior park.

artifact.description: PRD §X / plan FR-Y. Required (non-null, non-empty) when disposition=amendment_required — enforced fail-closed by the engine since the shared conflict subschema (also used by new_conflict, where artifact may be null) cannot express the per-disposition rule; may be null for new_conflict.

conflict.description: An object when disposition is amendment_required or new_conflict; null when proceeding. The object-vs-null rule is enforced fail-closed by the engine (steptypes._conflict_shape_error; see the top-level description / BOOTSTRAP-NOTES #46), NOT a schema allOf — the Anthropic structured-output API forbids top-level oneOf/allOf/anyOf.
